master_row.title: MACROSCOPIC FLUX QUANTIZATION Canonical Statutory Formula: Φ_mag
master_row.canonical_statement: [ID 1150] MACROSCOPIC FLUX QUANTIZATION Canonical Statutory Formula: $\Phi_{mag} = n \Phi_u \approx 4 \times 10^{22} \text{ quanta}$ Formal Technical Dissertation: Theorem 5. Extends quantum flux quantization ($\Phi_u = h/2e$) to planetary scales. It argues that Earth's magnetosphere is stabilized over geological timescales because the total flux is topologically locked to an integer number of quantum units. Source:
master_row.formation_note: Introduced in the source where [ID 1150] MACROSCOPIC FLUX QUANTIZATION Canonical Statutory Formula
master_row.dictionary: MACROSCOPIC FLUX QUANTIZATION Canonical Statutory Formula: $ _mag = n _u 4 10^22 quanta$ Formal Technical Dissertation: Theorem 5.
master_row.encyclopedia: MACROSCOPIC FLUX QUANTIZATION Canonical Statutory Formula: Φ_mag is a scaling / order-of-magnitude relation, written Φ_mag = n Φ_u ≈ 4 × 10^22 quanta. It expresses a scaling or proportionality, capturing how the quantity grows with its parameters. It is built from n, u, defining Φ_mag. Within the Trawin Zero Point registry it serves as one element of the framework's mathematical narrative.
